briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. We will now begin the climate briefing for March 10, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Global Climate Crisis: Accelerating Warming and Warning Signs
sections.content: Recent scientific studies confirm that global warming has rapidly accelerated over the past decade, warning that the Earth is incredibly close to breaching critical global warming targets [177, 183, 187]. This acceleration is manifesting as abnormal climate phenomena such as 'unprecedented' wet conditions or extended allergy seasons in Pennsylvania, while domestically, spring droughts following dry winters are being recognized as a 'warning sign' [1, 45, 178, 185]. Research also indicates that climate change could exacerbate inequality for women in the Asia-Pacific region and add billions of days of anxiety and depression in the United States, highlighting growing social impacts [180, 201]. Consequently, climate remediation conferences are calling for faster action and stronger leadership to address global warming, emphasizing the need for systematic adaptation policies [73, 184].

sections.title: 🌱 Strengthening Climate Change Response Leadership Among Domestic Companies
sections.content: Major domestic companies have demonstrated ESG management leadership by achieving outstanding results in CDP (Carbon Disclosure Project) climate change response evaluations. Samsung Electro-Mechanics was selected for the 'Platinum Club' a record 8 times among domestic companies, LG Innotek achieved 'Leadership A-grade' for three consecutive years, and LG Display earned 'Carbon Management Sector Honors' for 10 consecutive years, leading climate change response efforts in the electronic components industry [2, 7, 8, 9, 11, 14, 17, 19, 21, 24, 26, 27, 29, 30, 31, 32, 34, 37, 38, 39, 40, 42, 43, 44, 46, 47, 50, 51, 53, 54, 55, 56, 57, 58, 60, 63, 65, 67, 69, 70, 72, 74, 76, 79, 80, 84, 110, 120, 135, 166, 171, 172, 195]. In the financial sector, KB Financial Group achieved 'Platinum Club' or 'Leadership A-grade' for 9 consecutive years, and Shinhan Financial Group for 5 consecutive years, earning recognition as excellent companies in climate change response [2, 12, 15, 16, 22, 23, 28, 35, 41, 48, 49, 52, 59, 68, 75, 81, 82, 83, 85]. Korea Gas Corporation was the first energy public enterprise to receive a 'Leadership A- grade' in the climate change sector, setting an example for the public sector [18, 33, 36, 66, 78]. Furthermore, Hyundai Motor and Kia were recognized for their eco-friendly management performance, receiving awards in climate change and water resource management and launching the eco-friendly SUV 'The New Niro' [10, 25, 61, 71, 175].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Local Communities and Public Sector Efforts Towards a Carbon-Neutral Society
sections.content: Various local governments and public institutions in Korea are making diverse efforts to transition to a carbon-neutral society. Gimhae City has been selected for three consecutive years as a recipient of climate change support from the Ministry of Climate, Energy, and Environment [6, 64]. In Jeju Island, concrete actions are underway, including the launch of a 'tree-planting relay' activity for carbon neutrality and the introduction of Energy Storage Systems (ESS) in schools [92, 94, 114, 126]. Seoul City is expanding citizen-participatory climate crisis response education through its newly renovated 'Carbon Neutrality Experience Center' and 'Mobile Environmental Classroom' [89, 100, 127, 138, 140, 141]. Districts like Daejeon Seo-gu and Jecheon City are actively encouraging citizens to practice carbon reduction through environmental education for public officials and carbon neutrality point systems [90, 93, 95, 99, 102, 107, 112, 113, 125, 129, 132]. Notably, Busan City is strengthening its efforts for regional sustainable development by opening its 8th 'Our Neighborhood ESG Center,' a hub for carbon neutrality and age-tech [116, 121, 131, 139, 142].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Energy Transition and Policy Discussions
sections.content: Energy transition is a core pillar of climate change response, with active efforts to expand renewable energy and introduce new technologies both domestically and internationally. Jeonbuk Self-Governing Province announced its plan to usher in an era of 12GW of renewable energy through solar and offshore wind projects in the Saemangeum region [146, 147, 151]. International cooperation is also strengthening, with Korea Water Resources Corporation participating in a trillion-won project through water and renewable energy cooperation with the Philippines [145, 160]. However, concerns have been raised that domestic public finance invests 13 times more in fossil fuels than in renewable energy, necessitating discussions on the practical direction of energy transition policies [148, 149]. Notably, during the 'carbon neutrality public debate' process, the 'late reduction' scenario, which had been excluded from deliberation, was re-included as an option, sparking controversy and raising concerns about the transparency and consistency of the policy-making process [86, 124, 174]. While the importance of expanding investment in power grids and renewable energy is emphasized amidst recurring supply chain crises [150, 159], the top five domestic conglomerates are deepening their consideration of energy portfolio diversification, exploring avenues in SMR (Small Modular Reactor) and hydrogen energy, as they deem factory operations difficult with renewable energy alone [158].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted the severity of the climate crisis, along with various response efforts being made both domestically and internationally. We reiterate that continuous scientific observation, robust policy implementation, and the participation of civil society are essential for navigating the era of climate change. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: [Exclusive] 'Late-stage reduction model' — previously excluded from deliberation — re-emerges as an option; Carbon neutrality public discourse falters from the start
articles.original_title: [단독]숙의 절차서 뺀 ‘후기 감축형’ 다시 선택지로…탄소중립 공론화 시작부터 ‘삐걱’
articles.description: On April 23, 2024, stakeholders including plaintiffs of youth climate lawsuits, citizen climate lawsuits, infant climate lawsuits, and carbon neutrality basic plan lawsuits held a press conference in front of the Constitutional Court in Jongno-gu, Seoul. Reporter Jung Hyo-jin reports that the public deliberation process for amending the Framework Act on Carbon Neutrality and Green Growth for Climate Crisis Response (Carbon Neutrality Framework Act) is facing difficulties from the outset. The issue is that the 'late-stage reduction model' (convex type) reduction pathway, which the agenda deliberation group had decided to exclude, has been re-included as an option.

summary.phenomenon: On April 23, 2024, climate lawsuit stakeholders held a press conference in Jongno-gu, Seoul, and the public deliberation process for amending the Carbon Neutrality Framework Act is facing difficulties from the start. Specifically, the re-inclusion of the 'late-stage reduction model' (convex type) reduction pathway, which the agenda deliberation group had decided to exclude, has been identified as a problem.
